title: Dog Tremors From Moldy Food: Emergency Guide to Mycotoxin Poisoning
meta_desc: If your dog is shaking after eating moldy food, they may have mycotoxin poisoning. This is a high-urgency emergency. Learn the signs and next steps.
content_html:
TL;DR: Tremorgenic mycotoxin poisoning from moldy food is a life-threatening emergency that requires immediate veterinary intervention to manage severe neurological symptoms and seizures.

What causes tremorgenic mycotoxin poisoning in dogs?

When dogs consume moldy food—often scavenged from trash cans, compost bins, or forgotten pantry items—they may ingest tremorgenic mycotoxins. These are potent toxins produced by certain fungi that target the central nervous system, causing uncontrollable shaking, agitation, and neurological distress.

Is mycotoxin poisoning in dogs a medical emergency?

  • Urgency Level: High. This is a critical medical emergency that can lead to death if left untreated.
  • The condition can escalate rapidly from mild tremors to full-body seizures and life-threateningly high body temperatures (hyperthermia).
  • You should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately if you suspect your dog has eaten any moldy substances.

Why should I take a photo of the moldy food my dog ate?

  • A photo of the moldy food or the scavenging area helps the veterinary team confirm the likelihood of mold exposure and rule out other potential toxins.
  • This visual record speeds up the triage process, allowing for faster diagnosis.
  • Documentation ensures your dog receives the correct life-saving treatments, such as muscle relaxants or gastric decontamination, as quickly as possible.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Mycotoxin poisoning in dogs due to moldy feed, particularly high-protein foods, can cause tremors, polypnea, ataxia, collapse, and convulsive spasms. Tremors may also be secondary to muscle weakness. Diagnosis is based on a history of exposure and clinical signs. Differential diagnoses include ethylene glycol toxicosis, ingestion of hypotensive agents, and infectious diseases such as viral enteritis.

Chapter: Toxicology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 3011)

faqs.question: What are the first signs of mold poisoning in dogs?
faqs.answer: Initial symptoms often include restlessness, drooling, vomiting, and fine muscle tremors that may look like shivering or trembling.

faqs.question: How long after eating moldy food will a dog get sick?
faqs.answer: Symptoms typically begin within 30 minutes to 3 hours after ingestion, depending on the amount consumed and the dog's size.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: Do not induce vomiting unless specifically instructed by a veterinarian. If your dog is already showing neurological signs like tremors, they are at a high risk of inhaling vomit into their lungs (aspiration).
